rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE GWENN M. BENEDICT TRUST ESTABLISHED BY SUBSTITUTED JUDGMENT ON APRIL 6, 2001
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #6, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. The 6th and final account is allowed as filed. All reported acts of the successor trustee as set forth in the 6th account are allowed. Laura Brockwehl is awarded $4,509.50 as total compensation due for all services as trustee of the trust from 11/23/18 to 07/15/201 and she is authorized and directed to pay herself, $1,819.75 as remaining compensation. Laura Brockwehl is awarded up to $725.00 or 5 hours at $145 per hour for post accounting Trustee's fees to be paid from the tax reserve without further order of this court. Witherspoon & Sracusa, LLP is awarded $6,790.00 for all legal and paralegeal services provided to the trust from 8/8/19 to 10/1/21 and $426.40 in unreimbursed out of pocket costs. Witherspoon & Siracusa, LLP is awarded up to 5 hours in post account services from the tax reserve for anticipated additional legal and paralegal services without further Order of this court.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FRANZ VON UCKERMANN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/22/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The accounting period is alleged to be 1/5/19 - 1/4/21, but the attached accounting is for the period ending 7/31/20. The copies of property tax bills attached as Exhibit D to the petition appear to be duplicates of the bills for just the York Street property for the years 7/1/18 - 6/30/19 and 7/1/19 - 6/30/20 and support only the two disbursements made on 4/3/19 of $8,356.49 each and the two made on 12/4/19 of $8,647.73 each. None of the other tax payments is supported. The co-executors were appointed on 3/22/17. The order was not filed until 3/23/17. Letters Testamentary (not of Administration) were issued to them. The co-executors might want to mention that they were reinstated and when... It is still unclear why the real properties, or at least some of them, cannot be distributed now, when the co-executors themselves state that that would be in the estate's and the beneficiary's best interest. Paragraph 8.d. does not disclose how much in estate tax has been paid or when it was paid. Please see LRSF 14.34 D. and the referenced CRC 7.575(b) re bank statements and how to confirm that they are original. Please file a copy of the SFPUC water bill for $2,646.93. The copy of the letter from the SFPUC is cut off and does not appear to state any amount. Who is Ingeburg (Inge) Maier? (A pleading must stand on its own and should not "respond" to Examiner's Notes, which are not filed for the record or otherwise part of the file. For example, a reference to "this disbursement" in paragraph 36 of the petition makes no sense as it is entirely unclear which disbursement is meant.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 2/10/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ERNEST SMITH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The Grant of Probate attached as Exhibit D to the petition shows that Peter Heath, Sarah Louise Smith, Alison Jane Stent, and Joanne Michelle Heath were all appointed as Executors in Michael Heath's estate, not just Peter Heath. 2) The net amount of cash distributable to each heir appears to be slightly too low: $569,747.55 - $1,463.56 - ($14,708.46 x 2) - $150,000 = $388,867.07, 1/13th of which would be $29,912.85, not $29,879.38. 3) It would have been better had paragraph 7 of the prayer actually identified the successors-in-interest to the two post-deceased siblings rather than just stating that distribution would be made pursuant to [Probate Code] Section 13100 declarations on file, and, in the case of George Daniel Smith, had shown the proposed net distribution to each of his three successors-in-interest (presumably his sons). Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LINDA YEE LIN MA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/15/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 1/31/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF JEREMIAS ZEULI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the fourth supplement was filed beyond the deadline for when a courtesy copy of any response was to have been provided to the assigned Examiner, and NO courtesy of the third supplement was ever provided. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] The attorney fee declaration was never adequately supported or supplemented to meet the requirements of CRC 7.751(b). (A note to this effect will be made in the Court's records so that future fee requests not be approved unless fully supported.) A proposed order must not be part of a pleading; proposed orders must be submitted in hard copy, separately, for the judge to sign. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] A copy of the order will not be returned, as no SASE was provided.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WES LEE WINTERS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/22/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The AMENDMENT to the proposed distribution was not merely a "supplement" to the petition. [CRC 7.3] Notice is defective if mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. [LRSF 14.14 A.] The face of the Notice of Hearing states that the party "has filed" the document. The supplement was not E-filed until 12/28/21. The decedent died on 9/27/19, so interest started to accrue on the general pecuniary bequest as of 9/28/20, not 12/27/20. A pleading must stand on its own and not "respond" to Examiner's Notes, which are not filed for the record. An AMENDMENT to the petition must be filed that recites the correct proposed distribution to the heirs (divided in 8 shares at the nibling level), shows the amount of cash on hand, as of a date certain, and shows the calculation of the interest on the pecuniary bequest up to 2/22/22. Courtesy copies of the amendment and proof of service of notice mailed after the amendment is filed, plus a revised proposed order, must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 2/14/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B. (Merely typing in a date on the waivers of account that were not fully executed with dates, does not make them complete or fully executed. As long as all heirs get proper notice, the incomplete waivers of account will be overlooked.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES EDWARD DOHERTY JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: continued to 2/22/22 (first available date). as stated in the examiner notes, the supplement was to have been signed and verified by petitioner, but it was signed and verified by an attorney. the exception that allows an attorney to sign and verify on behalf of a client does not apply when the client is seeking appointment as a fiduciary (CRC 7.103(c)). the examiner notes, also, that there is reference to the County of Contra Costa below the caption, and #2 says "Petiitoner is not seeking appointment with bond . . .is requesting a bond of $175,000". the client must verify the supplement. the issue of notice to the residents of 55 Laguna Open House will have to be resolved by the judicial officer. please note, examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.
